decisions: No content to prune or reorganize: reviewed 8 files (core/MISTAKES.md, core/PREFERENCES.md, core/LEARNINGS.md, SUMMARY.md, TODAY.md, daily/2026-04-21.md, daily/2026-04-22.md, daily/2026-04-23.md) — all contain placeholder text or consolidation-only entries.
Previous recommendation from reflection-2026-04-21 reviewed: 'no gaps yet, brain is new' — confirmed still accurate after 3 days with zero user interactions.
observations: All memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain at bootstrap-empty state after 4 days of operation
Daily log chain is healthy (04-21 through 04-24) but contains exclusively maintenance activity — no user-generated content has entered the pipeline
Self-assessment pass rates are inflated by trivially-true criteria on an empty brain — this is a known limitation, not a quality issue
recommendations: First user interaction: ensure the session produces at least one entry in TODAY.md and that the next consolidation correctly promotes it to the appropriate tier. Success criterion: after the first real user session + consolidation, at least one of core/semantic/episodic/procedural contains a non-placeholder entry derived from the session.
Consider adding a staleness alert if the brain reaches 7 days with no user interactions — this would surface potential configuration or routing issues
processImprovements: [self-critique] The reflection process is running on schedule but cannot meaningfully assess memory quality when all tiers are empty. The 'gap-impact-analysis' criterion passes because I identified that empty tiers mean consolidation has nothing to validate — but this is a weak signal. The reflection adds value only when there is content to reflect on.

[proposal] Add a 'days-since-last-user-session' counter to the reflection report to make idle periods explicitly visible. This would help distinguish 'healthy but unused' from 'broken routing/configuration'. Rationale: 4 days idle could be normal for a new agent, but 14 days idle likely signals a problem.
[blocked] Cannot validate the promotion pipeline (daily → tier) without a real user session. This is an operational dependency, not something fixable in the maintenance process itself.
